justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and two vehicles while ground truth contains four pedestrians and six vehicles, so many entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes provided for detected entities are reasonable (actions, context, motion and door/trunk states) but many entities and some attributes are missing, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some useful relations are reported (pedestrian left of truck, parked on left, car moving towards camera) but relations are sparse and many object relationships are absent.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some correct attributes for a subset of objects but is incomplete overall with limited spatial coverage, yielding a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: Missing one of two pedestrians and most vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (age, actions, open/closed states) but misses many objects and motion details, and some motion labels conflict with ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few relations (left/behind) but relations are sparse and not fully consistent or comprehensive relative to scene objects.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially informative but incomplete and has inconsistencies in object coverage and motion labeling.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and two of four vehicles, missing several objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes (actions, age, trunk/door states, track hints) beyond the ground truth but omits object IDs and several motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives plausible relations (pedestrian left of truck, car behind truck) for reported objects but misses relations for the omitted objects, reducing coverage.
justifications.overall: Moderate performance: correct attribute extraction for reported entities but incomplete detections and missing mappings to ground-truth objects limit reliability.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian (of four) and two vehicles (of five) were reported, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Per-object attributes (actions, age, motion status, doors/trunk) are correctly provided for detected objects but many objects are missing and vehicle types are coarse.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations (near building, next to truck, blocking road, behind truck) add useful context but cannot be fully validated against the GT and include a speculative claim about blocking the road.
justifications.overall: Partial detection with generally correct attributes for detected entities but low recall and some speculative spatial claims lead to a moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses all pedestrians and several vehicles, reporting only two vehicles while omitting multiple ground truth objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful per-vehicle attributes like type, motion, and door/trunk status but omits actor actions, ages, and many object instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a couple of plausible spatial relations but they are sparse, not comprehensive, and not clearly grounded to all detected objects.
justifications.overall: Some correct vehicle attribute extraction but major omissions of pedestrians and additional vehicles lead to low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only one of six pedestrians and two of seven vehicles, missing most objects and many attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided attributes like action and age for the single detected pedestrian and motion/type for vehicles, but missed key attributes (for example carrying and phone) and many objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse relations to the shop are given and one pedestrian context matches ground truth, but most spatial contexts such as jaywalking or walking along the roadside are absent.
justifications.overall: Overall the output is sparse and only partially correct, offering limited usable semantic information due to many missing entities and omitted attributes.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports 1 pedestrian and 2 vehicles versus 3 pedestrians and 6 vehicles in the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple per-object attributes (atomic actions, age, types, motion status, trunk/doors) giving reasonably rich semantics for reported objects despite limited coverage.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided and appear plausible for the listed objects, but many scene objects lack any spatial annotations and relations cannot be fully validated against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Although the reported objects have detailed attributes, the extraction omits most pedestrians and vehicles from the scene, resulting in a low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 1 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 6 vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides action, context, and age for one person and type/motion for two vehicles but misses transporting for the carrying person and other object attributes, so moderate detail but incomplete.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several relations that are mutually consistent (person near white truck; truck next to person; car behind truck) but relations are sparse and do not cover most objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some correct attributes and plausible spatial relations for a few objects but misses the majority of pedestrians and vehicles, yielding a below-average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only two pedestrians and two vehicles were extracted out of seven persons and five vehicles in the ground truth, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction lists basic attributes like atomic action and age but misses many ground truth contextual labels such as jaywalking, carrying and communicative actions, and lacks track identifiers.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but are sparse, possibly imprecise, and do not cover most ground truth objects or explicit relative positions.
justifications.overall: Moderate-to-low performance due to limited object coverage and missing important semantic labels despite some correct basic attributes and spatial hints.

justifications.completeness: Contains only one pedestrian and one vehicle while ground truth lists seven persons and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields but misses many ground-truth labels (e.g., specific simple contexts, transporting and communicative cues) and mislabels contexts, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers basic relations between the detected person and vehicle but omits relations for most objects and appears inconsistent with ground-truth contexts.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures a few attributes but is largely incomplete and contains incorrect or missing semantic and spatial information, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Covers only 2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of annotated objects and thus largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (communicative, transporting, age) but contains inaccuracies and misassignments compared to ground truth, yielding moderate richness but low reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations which add context but they are somewhat vague and largely unverifiable because the ground truth lacks explicit spatial labels.
justifications.overall: Low coverage of objects combined with several semantic errors results in an overall low quality extraction.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles, missing the majority of objects and some important attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic action and age labels but omits many contextual and transport/communicative details present in the ground truth and lacks track identifiers.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers a few pairwise spatial relations, but they are sparse, not comprehensive, and not tied to ground-truth IDs so grounding and coverage are limited.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partial and noisy with limited coverage of objects and semantic attributes, resulting in low overall utility for perception evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and a single vehicle while missing several persons and vehicles, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some attributes (actions, age, door/trunk states) but misses many actors and shows label/motion mismatches, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes coarse relations to shops but relations are vague and some pedestrian contexts and vehicle motion/location information do not align with the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Captures basic actions and a few spatial cues but lacks coverage and contains inaccuracies, resulting in below-average overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of objects and many per-object attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic fields (actions, context, age, transport, door/trunk status) but omits many GT-specific attributes and contains labeling inconsistencies (e.g., vehicle type and motion status mismatches).
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some coarse spatial relations for a few objects but lacks correspondence to GT IDs, omits relations for most entities, and may be imprecise relative to the scene.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partial and somewhat semantically detailed but misses most objects and contains notable inaccuracies in types, motion statuses, and spatial grounding.

justifications.completeness: Covers only 2 of 5 annotated pedestrians and 2 of 5 annotated vehicles, missing the majority of objects in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some useful attributes (atomic action, age, vehicle type) but omits important labels (e.g., communicative for one pedestrian) and misrepresents or omits many vehicle motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations that could be helpful but they are sparse, partially ungrounded to annotated IDs, and do not cover most objects.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially accurate but substantially incomplete and limited in semantic detail, resulting in low overall utility for the full scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected 2 of 6 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles with many missing objects and no track IDs.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attribute extraction is partially correct (actions and ages mostly correct) but misses communicative cues and misstates simple contexts for several pedestrians and lacks vehicle IDs to verify types.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are coarse and in some cases inconsistent with ground truth contexts and cannot be fully verified due to missing IDs.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some basic attributes but omits most objects, mislabels contextual relations, and lacks alignment to ground truth IDs, yielding overall low quality.

justifications.completeness: Extracted 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles, missing several actors and many vehicle instances/states, so largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (age, action, simple context) but misses important labels like jaywalking and group communication and omits many vehicle details.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few relative positions but uses vague phrasing and fails to provide spatial relations for most objects, limiting precision.
justifications.overall: Captures basic scene elements but is incomplete and missing key semantic and spatial details, resulting in limited reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of roughly 6 vehicles with some misclassified actions, so coverage of relevant objects is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes (age, atomic action, door/trunk state) but misses many important labels (most pedestrians marked walking, communicative cues) and contains incorrect action labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reports a small set of consistent relative relations (e.g., person right of van, van left of person) but relations are sparse and lack verifiable linkage to ground truth track IDs.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is limited by many missed entities and some incorrect semantics despite reasonable but incomplete spatial relation reporting.

justifications.completeness: Only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 4 vehicles were reported, missing the majority of ground-truth actors.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several attributes are incorrect or invented, making the richness moderate but unreliable.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some plausible spatial relations and vehicle motion statuses are provided and internally consistent, but mappings to ground-truth objects are incomplete and some relations cannot be verified.
justifications.overall: Due to low coverage of pedestrians, mixed attribute accuracy, and partial spatial information, the extraction has limited completeness and moderate semantic content.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 6 ground-truth persons (33%) and 2 of 4 ground-truth vehicles (50%), yielding a low overall object coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides several attribute fields (age, actions, vehicle states) but misses or mislabels key semantics (omits multiple communicative labels and incorrectly adds a transporting label) so the attribute quality is moderate-to-poor.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction offers a few relative spatial claims but the ground truth contains no spatial annotations to validate against and the relations are sparse and potentially inconsistent, so confidence is low.
justifications.overall: Given the limited recall of objects, some incorrect semantic claims, and unverifiable sparse spatial relations, the extraction is of low to moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians out of four and one vehicle out of four, missing multiple objects and many object-level attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captured basic attributes like walking and age for pedestrians and motion/trunk/doors for a vehicle but missed specific context labels such as jaywalking and omitted several object instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a couple of vague spatial cues (near the shops, to the left of the pedestrians) but omits more precise relations and relations for many objects, limiting usefulness.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is partially correct but incomplete and lacking important contextual and spatial detail, yielding low to moderate utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two of four pedestrians and missed two moving vehicles while adding an extra bicycle, so many objects are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes but misses important contextual details like jaywalking and vehicle motion for two cars, and includes some irrelevant labels.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations fields are empty despite implicit cues in track hints, so structured spatial relationships are not captured.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some basic attributes but misses multiple actors, important contextual semantics, and structured spatial relations, yielding a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Missed one of three pedestrians and two moving vehicles, reporting only two pedestrians and one parked bicycle, so largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic attributes like age and atomic action but omits critical labels (e.g., jaywalking) and motion status for moving vehicles, limiting semantic depth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes some relations (on the road, near shops, next to the sidewalk) but misses jaywalking/crossing relations and lacks relations for one pedestrian and moving vehicles.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures basic elements but misses several actors and key contextual labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Many entities are missing from the extraction, with only 2 of 6 pedestrians detected and one of the two moving vehicles omitted, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many attribute fields (age, motion, doors/trunk) but mislabels important contextual semantics like jaywalking and omits several entities, yielding moderate richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided but are coarse, sometimes inconsistent with ground truth contexts (e.g., labeling jaywalking subjects as walking along the side) and are missing for many objects.
justifications.overall: The output captures some correct attributes and a few vehicle motion states but misses the majority of pedestrians and key contextual labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only half of the pedestrians (2 of 4) and missed one moving vehicle, so several ground-truth entities and critical context labels are absent.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes many attribute fields (action, age, communicative, transporting, vehicle type) but misses critical contextual labels like jaywalking and misstates some scene context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides useful relative relations between agents and scene elements, though these relations are partially coarse and some are unverifiable against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Extraction is partially informative with moderate semantic detail but incomplete detection and missing safety-relevant context, yielding an overall middling quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 5 ground-truth pedestrians and 2 of 3 ground-truth vehicles, missing multiple pedestrians and at least one moving vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides key attributes (atomic actions, age, motion status) accurately for detected actors but omits complex contextual labels (e.g., 'jaywalking'), fails to capture some nuanced simple contexts, and misses many actors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations (e.g., 'behind pedestrians', 'next to the shop') but they are vague, inconsistently applied, and absent for most ground-truth actors and context details.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is limited by low recall of actors and incomplete context annotation despite generally correct attributes for the detected objects.

justifications.completeness: The extraction only lists one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains five pedestrians and three vehicles and omits track identifiers.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides a variety of attributes (actions, context, age, motion and door/trunk states) plus track hints and spatial relations, but it misses many entities and includes an unverified vehicle type.

justifications.overall: While the extraction contains meaningful semantic labels for the detected objects, it is largely incomplete relative to ground truth and includes uncertain spatial claims, resulting in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of four pedestrians and misidentified both ground-truth moving vehicles as a single parked van, so most entities are missing or incorrect.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some attributes (age, action, doors/trunk) but misses many actors and mislabels vehicle motion, reducing overall attribute usefulness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers vague spatial cues for the reported objects but omits relations for most pedestrians and mislocates vehicles relative to ground truth motion.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing: the extraction lists 2 pedestrians versus 6 in the ground truth and only 1 vehicle versus 2, so completeness is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides rich per-person and per-vehicle attributes (actions, context, communicative, transporting, age) but several entities and some context details are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for the detected entities but cannot be fully verified because the ground truth contains no explicit spatial annotations and relations for many missing pedestrians are absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction supplies useful semantic labels for detected objects but misses the majority of entities and includes spatial relations that are not verifiable from the ground truth, resulting in limited overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction detects only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 1 of 2 vehicles, missing multiple objects and mislabeling at least one pedestrian's action, so coverage is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (age, actions, context, vehicle door/trunk states) indicating moderate richness, but lacks object IDs and contains some incorrect attribute values.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial relations for reported entities that are partially plausible (sidewalk, in front of shops, middle of road) but conflict with ground-truth actions/contexts for some pedestrians.

justifications.completeness: Extracted only 2 of 7 pedestrians and omitted several on-road and jaywalking instances, though the vehicle is reported.

justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided but are often incorrect or incomplete, assigning sidewalks where several ground-truth actors are on the road or jaywalking.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some attributes of visible actors but omits most pedestrians and mislabels spatial context, limiting its reliability for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 8 pedestrians though correctly reported the single vehicle, so most person instances are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracts useful attributes (actions, simple context, age, vehicle state) but omits track IDs and important complex/contextual labels like jaywalking and many instances.

justifications.completeness: Many objects are missing: one person (biker) and two vehicles (including the stopped vehicle) are not reported, so coverage is poor.

justifications.overall: Captures some basic object attributes correctly but misses multiple objects and key contextual details, yielding a below-average overall extraction.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses multiple annotated pedestrians and several vehicles including one stopped vehicle, so it is largely incomplete.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and one of three vehicles, missing key actors and a moving vehicle.

justifications.completeness: Extraction finds only two of four vehicles and mislabels or omits key pedestrian actions and contexts, so coverage is low.

justifications.completeness: Misses the moving vehicle and mislabels one person's atomic action, but otherwise detects both persons and one vehicle.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but omitted all vehicles and misclassified one pedestrian's atomic action, so only partially complete.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but only one of four vehicles was reported, so many scene elements are missing.

justifications.completeness: Detected both pedestrians but missed multiple vehicles and did not label the bicyclist action, so coverage is partial.

justifications.completeness: Misses the cyclist entirely and reports only a single vehicle while the ground truth lists multiple vehicles, so the extraction is largely incomplete.

justifications.completeness: Misses the annotated person (cyclist) and most vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only a single vehicle.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only two pedestrians and no vehicles, missing most annotated persons and all annotated vehicles from the ground truth.
